layman_headline: Market fear is high but no coin has a clean enough setup to justify pulling the trigger today.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful mood with most altcoins trending down while Bitcoin dominance stays elevated at 56.5%. Several coins look beaten up — BONK is down 25% in a week, and ETH's RSI is approaching oversold territory — but none of the candidates present a clean technical setup with confirmed reversal signals. Jumping in now without a clear bounce confirmation would be catching a falling knife. The best approach is to wait for a stronger entry signal.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. Keep cash ready and watch for ETH to dip toward $1,750 with a clear bounce pattern before entering. Patience pays in fearful markets.
expert_reasoning: While ETH's RSI at 35 is approaching oversold (a historically reliable signal per our 80% win rate on RSI trades), the chart structure is still bearish — price below all major moving averages with a risk/reward of only 0.36x to nearest levels. The Bullish Marubozu candle shows buying conviction, but BTC dominance at 56.5% is hostile territory for alt entries. LDO's +24.5% weekly surge with RSI still at 41 is intriguing but could easily retrace. BONK's -25% weekly drop with chart bias of -1.4 is pure capitulation with no reversal confirmation. The prudent play is to wait for ETH to test the $1,700-$1,750 support zone and form a higher low before deploying capital.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to crush altcoin performance — capital rotation into alts not yet confirmed
FTX's $900M distribution round could create sustained selling pressure across major tokens
Fear & Greed at 27 is improving from 25 but could easily reverse if macro headwinds intensify
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,750-$1,700 support zone — a bounce here with volume would trigger a BUY
Fear & Greed Index trajectory — if it pushes above 30 it signals sentiment recovery for alt entries
LDO momentum continuation — if it holds above $0.35 after its 24.5% weekly rally, it becomes a strong candidate
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear phase with BTC flat at $64K while alts bleed. High BTC dominance at 56.5% signals capital is not flowing into altcoins yet, making alt swing trades higher risk than normal.
context_banner: Fear-driven market with no confirmed reversal signals — discipline demands patience over premature entries.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 approaching oversold — historically our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but needs confirmation of support before acting.
Fear & Greed at 27 improving from 25, suggesting sentiment may be bottoming but not yet confirming a reversal.
FTX distributing $900M to creditors could create short-term selling pressure across the market, making it wise to wait for the impact to play out.
ETH derivatives reset narrative suggests clearing of overleveraged positions, which could set up a cleaner bounce once complete.
No candidate showed a chart bias above +0.4, and most are in bearish structure below key moving averages — insufficient conviction for entry.
